This is more so something you want to do with something that's at least like 5 to 10K. Ideally, it's like an investment based product or service that you're selling.

[22:40]
"So like the the main thing that I love to explain as an example with uh with risk adjusted returns, it's like the infamous uh blackjack table or Russian roulette, right? So you can get an amazing return if you put $1,000 on red, but it's one of the riskiest things that you could do."
[23:00]
"So when you take into consideration the amount of risk that you have to trade for that return, it's a shitty quote unquote investment. At that point, you're just gambling. So the point is with these types of like Jeremy said, money based offers or ROI based offers, you want to be really transparent that there's risk associated with every investment."
[23:25]
"But then you're you're you're basically condensing the amount of risk and justifying why it's logical to trade that amount of risk for the amount of upside of this investment. [24:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Pogo Stick Technique"
NÚMEROS: $10,000 exemplo de preço, $50K upfront cost
VISUAL: Conceito visual de "pogo stick" - saltar do preço direto para onboarding
[24:20]
okay, now this I I call this the pogo stick. Okay, so ask me what my price is, Nick. Say, "Hey, what's your price?"
"What's your price?"
"$10,000. That's where most people stop. They're shit weirdos. It's like, that is not how you close. It's like you want to say, it's like the pogo stick, meaning we're going to say the price, but then we're going to move into what happens after they pay whenever that question's asked. Okay. Okay.
[24:55]
So, it's like here we're going to say, "Okay, so step one, you're going to invest with us at 50k upfront cost." Okay. Right after that happens, here's what occurs. And I'm going to go through my onboarding process with them. Okay? And I'm going to talk about what the steps are that come after the investment to get the result. That make sense? Pogo stick.
[25:20]
Do not stop on the price. There's no slide in here where it's just a price and that's it. It's like we bring it up and we talk about what happens after. Okay? Pogo stick the price. Does that make sense? Okay.

VISUAL: Social proof, credibility markers, trust indicators
[28:05]
So, company credibility slide. Um, same thing. Typically with any kind of big uh uh you know, investment that you're attempting to solicit from people, you're going to want to add something that you know really lets people know that you're the ship and that they can trust you and that other people have already trusted you.
[28:30]
Um, you know, if you want an example of this, go to California. Any any person you ever talk to in California will not do a deal with you unless like five of their friends will tell you that. Yeah, I know that guy. He's cool. Okay, this is this is how you got to act in terms of the internet.
[28:50]
Meaning, you got to let people know that others that they might already know have either trusted you or that once again like x amount of investors have trusted you with x amount of money or x amount of clients or x amount of students have trusted you with x amount of revenue. Does that make sense?
[29:15]
We want to show transparently once again like I I tell clients all the time, I'm like, "Yeah, so this is my price. This is the rev share we do and we offer a guarantee." And by the way, like I had a guy just the other day, his name's Dave. So he's teaching people how to do laundry mats. Didn't work out after two months. We ended up refunding him.
[29:40]
You know, it's like so we also transparently say like, "Look, a deal didn't work out. We refunded the guy." You know, if you want to talk to him, he's still a friend. But yeah, just didn't work. So we gave him his money back. Like that's the kind of stuff we'd want to say if we offer a guarantee as an example that would increase credibility with our company. Does that make sense? Okay.
[30:05]
Some of you obviously have wildly different hard flexes for your company credibility. Like some of you are literally backed by like substantial amounts of revenue or some of you are backed by you know some partner that like people know that and you're unknown so you leverage their partnership for your authority right
[30:30]
some of you are like you know you got x amount of students or I I commonly say like I've got 3,800 plus paid students that lets people know who are going to buy from me as a student that like look there's already been literally thousands of other people that have done this like happily to connect you.
[30:50]
We post their names like with testimonials and people look those people up all the time and reach out and ask like, "Hey, what'd you get from this course?" You know, that kind of stuff because we're transparent. Does that make sense? All right.
[31:10]
You had you had a question, Christian?
PERGUNTA: "Yeah. Um, so would like a clients we've worked with or worked with kind of slide the good for credibility here or special specifically like stories about clients we've worked with?"
[31:25]
Yeah, that can that can work. Uh, it's really a combination, right? And it's different for every person. It's like you selling SEO and ads as an example. Some company credit bill like I spend anywhere from at this point as low as 800K. Like I had a client last month spent $2.2 million.
[31:50] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Credibility Examples"

VISUAL: Exemplos específicos de credibilidade, números de clientes e gastos

[32:15]
It's not necessarily just, you know, oh, I have 10 clients. It's like, okay, you know, like whatever. like you're do you're doing multiple way figures in a month in returns, you know, like, okay, and then I can prove it, too. Like, I'll I'll happily show all that stuff. [52:30]

[57:20]
The hydras like imagine you saw this intimidating monster in real life. It's extremely aggressive. You know, it's going to go at you until you die. So that's what we want to do here. We want to collect the lead and we literally want to get them to buy or unsubscribe. Like we want to absolutely fucking hammer them. Okay?
[57:45]

[58:10] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Sales Team Speed Strategy"
NÚMEROS: 20 closers notification blast
VISUAL: Google Sheet lead claiming system, incentivo à velocidade vs. round robin
[58:15]
Meaning we want to add them into a Google. This is what I do. Okay? I don't do this for Sean. I do this for the other people I do the hydro with. Soon as a lead comes in, I I I send an email blast to every closer on the team with the information and I say to claim this lead, go into the sheet and mark it as assigned to you.
[58:45]
That's the only way they can claim the lead. And it says you can only claim the lead if you can contact them right now. So I'll blast it out to like 20 shit closers. Okay? One will be able to claim the lead.
[59:05]
And and I do this intentionally because round robins do not incentivize speed. We need to reach out to these people fast and aggressively. We got to reach out to them quick.
[59:25]
So, moral of the story is, as soon as I get that lead, immediate notification goes out to every closer. I don't give a shit who gets it. I don't care if one closer gets all of them because he's the one picking up the phone and contacting everybody in real time. That's what I want. I want to incentivize speed if I roundroin it.
[59:50] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Case Study - Rico's Amazon Automation"
NÚMEROS: 3 closers (Brian, Marty, Aaron), $40K liquid leads
VISUAL: Exemplo de competição entre closers, Brian reclamando vs. Marty e Aaron claiming leads
[59:55]
So, so I did this the other day. There's a client called Rico. He sells Amazon automation. And Rico has three closers, okay? Brian, Marty, and Aaron. So, anyway, I call all three of them. and I explained that exact thing I just said and initially they were very open to it. Like they were like, "Yeah, cool. Like, let's do it."
[1:00:25]
Uh, one out of three of them, Brian was bitching about it. Like a couple hours later, I go into the sheet. He hadn't claimed one lead. Marty and Aaron had claimed all the leads. He's over here bitching like, "Oh, we should put it in a round robin."
[1:00:45]
I'm like, "No, no, look at how shit you're being." I was like, "You are saying this?" I was like, "You're saying this because you're not claiming any other leads and they're shit out claiming them." I was like, "And you also see that they're saying they have 40k liquid and are fucking ready to buy now." I was like, "That's why you're pissed."
[1:01:10]
I was like, "I'm not doing a round robin." I was like, "This is exactly what the system's supposed to do. Get get better. Use your use your emotion here to be faster instead. I'm not round robbing him."
[1:01:30]
So anyway, moral of the story, that's a part of this and you'll you'll read that in here just to be clear. And there's also it's also a screen capture of the lead notification uh on one of the last pages here where you can see like what I specifically type with what I just said. Okay.
[1:01:50]
PERGUNTA: "In order to claim the lead the the lead needs to answer or no?"
No. No. So if you look at that right there, it says like, "Hey, click on this link to the Google sheet." There's a column in the Google sheet that says assigned to. All they have to do is go in there and type their name and that's their lead.
[1:02:15]
I also put just to talk about some of the technical stuff. When you have your salespeople do this, in fairness, the person could book a call right after filling out the form. And then the call is likely going to round robin, of course. Okay?
[1:02:35]
So, you have to have a VA that like is in the sheet taking leads that may have been scheduled to somebody else and and changing that person who it's scheduled with to the person who got it assigned to them. So, I have a I have a cheap VA that does that. Does that make sense? Do you understand what I'm saying here? It's very important. You understand what I just said?
[1:03:00]
"When the first puller played that lead."
So, so let's say let's say you get the notification. It's like Joel goes in and he says Joel. Okay. But that lead then pressed the button to book a call and it booked with Jake. Okay. I'm going to have my VA go in and he's going to be like, "Okay, I see a booked call with Jake. Let me just double check that in the sheet. Oh, looks like Joel's the one who claimed that lead."
[1:03:35]
Yeah. Exactly. So, now he's going to take that scheduled call that's in the future like 24 to 48 hours out because calls never happen the same day when they're booked. That VA is gonna be able to easily go in there once a day and just shit move all the calls to whoever they were assigned to.
[1:03:55]
Yeah, that's correct. So, yeah. So, I just have the VA going and clean everything up. So, like in your system, as an example for your Hydra, it's like I know you don't do this, but like to be clear, this would incentivize speed for those closures that you're about to bring on.

[1:08:15]

[1:37:10]

[1:45:45] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Communication Preference Split"
NÚMEROS: 50/50 phone vs. text preference, $40K liquid qualifier
VISUAL: Divisão de preferência de contato, adaptação baseada em resposta
[1:45:50]
So, then based on whether they said yes to having 40k liquid or not, and whether they wanted phone or text, the closer would still call and text either way. However, we typically find that the person based on what they answered with was how they chose to communicate with us.
[1:46:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Modern Buying Behavior"
NÚMEROS: $35K Louis Vuitton watch purchased via text
VISUAL: Exemplo de compra high-ticket sem ligação telefônica, apenas texto
[1:46:20]
People, so the Louis V watch I spent 35k on, did I talk to the guy on the phone? Did I see it in person? I literally didn't. I bought it all over text. Didn't talk to the guy one time. He sent me a photo of it. Sent me a video of it. I was like, "Yeah, that's dope." He sent me another video about it. I was like, "Here's my card." And yeah, didn't even see it. You know,
[1:46:50]
uh this house didn't even come see it. Just like facetimed me, showed me how it looked. I was like, "Yeah, it looks sick. Give me the lease." You know, you get what I'm saying? Buying behavior is very different now than it used to be. Okay?
[1:47:10]
So, just remember that. Like, you might be thinking, same thing. It's we gota we got to crush out these shit ideas closers have. Oh, nobody ever buys via text. It's like, shut your shit mouth. [1:47:35]
Like, you can use that example I just described. And I can give you plenty more of people who buy multi-10,000 items that are not making money that over text message without talking to anybody. [1:48:10] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Hydra Summary"
NÚMEROS: 2-3 week closer follow-up window, 200+ leads per day capacity
VISUAL: Resumo completo da estratégia Hydra
[1:48:15]
I'm just going to summarize real quick. The hydra Facebook lead form. Okay. Instagram lead form. We could do this on LinkedIn. We could do this on Tik Tok. Uh, anything that has a lead form, we can do it on an ad channel. Okay?
[1:48:35]
We're going to funnel all that shit into a Google sheet and into their CRM. Okay? We're going to send a notification when a lead comes in to the closers. Whoever gets it assigned to them and actually contacts them is the person who gets the lead.
[1:48:55]
A VA is going to clean up the list for any other calls that may have been scheduled or anybody else in the CRM that may already be assigned to. Okay. After that happens, we are going to have marketing automation be including emails and text messages that shit hammer these people until they schedule a call.
[1:49:20]
That's my call to action by the way in the marketing automation. If it's a call funnel, I'm pushing them to book a call. Okay? I'm pushing them to talk to somebody. Okay.

the sales people at the same time while I'm doing my marketing automation hammer them are also hammering them okay the length of time does not matter we want them to close okay so we will continue hammering them until closed
[1:50:10]

Okay.
[1:50:40]
That way, that way they retain the ability to follow up with everybody that comes in over the course of the following days. Does that make sense? You have questions on this?
[1:51:10]
PERGUNTA: "Have you tried that with like with high output? Because for examp the Facebook like super bad quality like leads."
I'll tell you this just to be very clear. I had the same exact belief, but when Sean told me that he raised $150 million through Facebook lead forms, I don't want to be right. I just want to make money.
[1:51:40]
There's a very common human I I don't remember the name of the uh the cognitive bias that we hold. Uh cognitive dissonance. No, no, no, no. Anyway, long story short, there's a specific cognitive bias that we hold that essentially based on like past data to us where we've made like firm decisions about how things are in life. We want those to remain firm.
[1:52:10]
Otherwise, it kind of like undoes our foundation for how we operate as humans and like that doesn't feel good. Like we want certainty. So, moral of the story, like I I totally agree with what you're saying to be clear, and I I've literally thought the same thing.
[1:52:35]
And I have no incentive here other than to help make you more money for you to just be open to trying it because as soon as I tried it, it worked. And then I rolled it out again and it worked. And then I rolled it out again and it worked. Then I rolled it out again, it worked.
[1:53:00]
The only time it didn't work was the one client I said that didn't contact the people. And I've been getting people, as I mentioned, 54% of people saying yes, they have 100k liquid. And they're not lying.
[1:53:20]
So, I just want to be fair in saying if they have 100k liquid and I'm closing to people at that kind of level, I I believe that you'll be able to sell a shit FAHA through it to a girl who just got a BBL, you know? I can't I can't imagine it's going to be hard in comparison to like what we're selling through the same exact thing. Does that make sense? Yeah.
[1:53:50]
So, be open to it just in case you have that same bias. Uh cuz cuz like I said, I definitely did, too. All right, cool. Any other questions? All right, lunch is in the kitchen. We'll uh take an hour. I'll round everybody up when we're ready again.
📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO
NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:
Mini Webinar 2.0:
$12M+ em vendas geradas
3.5 meses de operação
$25K-$100K faixa de preço típica
$5K-$10K mínimo recomendado
$100K price point (exemplo trucking automation)
20-25 minutos duração recomendada
2x velocidade de reprodução opcional
3,800+ estudantes pagos (exemplo de credibilidade)
$800K-$2.2M gastos mensais em ads (exemplo credibilidade)
The Hydra Strategy:
$150M levantados via Facebook lead forms (Sean)
54% taxa de resposta "sim" para $100K liquid
Flutuação atual: 40s%
800+ leads testados
$10 custo por lead qualificado
$600-$1,000 custo original por call (mini webinar)
$11 CPMs
$1-$3 custo por lead
3:1 ROAS imediato (só lead form)
4.5+ ROAS com email marketing
$500K gasto em 4 semanas (meta Own Boss Supply Co)
3 emails por dia (vs. 1 a cada 2 dias)
$20-60K revenue por email
400K lista de emails
3 textos por dia
$20-80K revenue por texto
$50-$200 budget diário inicial recomendado
$5-$20 custo por lead objetivo
$1-$5 custo por lead ideal
4 closers → 30 closers (scaling necessário)
$1,900 gasto, $120K cash collected, $210K contract value (Robert exemplo)
2-3 semanas janela de follow-up para closers
200+ leads por dia capacidade
1 em 4 conversão (fence/deck caso)
$12K ad spend, $622K revenue (fence/deck)
$170K aumento de revenue (fence/deck)
15 minutos para booking de appointment
$35K Louis Vuitton watch (comprado via texto)
$20K Dubai residency offer (exemplo de peak interest perdido)
$60K/ano salário corporativo exemplo
ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:
Mini Webinar 2.0 Structure:
Slide 1: Explicação simples da oferta
Slide 2: Dados de terceiros validando a indústria
Slide 3: Comparação de investimentos (onde colocar dinheiro)
Slide 4: Cash flow vs. tax incentive comparison
Slide 5: A oferta completa (bullet points + preço)
Slides 6-7: Math breakdown (P&L, income statements, ROI)
Slide 8: Risk adjusted cash on cash return
Slides 9-10: Testimonials
Slide 11: Onboarding process (Pogo Stick Technique)
Slide 12: Transição para objeções
Slides 13-15: Handling de objeções (time frame, credibility, profit share)
Slide 16: Money back guarantee
Slides 17-18: Mais testimonials relacionados a objeções
Slide 19: Reiteração da oferta
Slide 20: How to get started process
Slides 21-22: Testimonials finais + CTA final
The Pogo Stick Technique:
Nunca parar no preço
Sempre seguir: Preço → O que acontece depois do pagamento
Integrar onboarding no momento de revelar investimento
The Hydra Strategy (Multi-Head Dragon):
Facebook Lead Form: 2 perguntas qualificadoras
Push to: Mini webinar OU página de vendas
Lead Capture: Email, nome, telefone
Google Sheet System: Closers claiming leads em tempo real
Speed Incentive: Primeiro a clamar e contactar ganha o lead
VA Cleanup: Reorganiza calls agendadas para quem clamou
Marketing Automation Head:
Email imediato de confirmação
3 emails por dia (educação/case studies/ofertas)
3 textos por dia via PostScript
Voicemail drops
Sales Team Head:
Notificação blast para todos os closers
Contato imediato (não esperar)
Múltiplos touchpoints (5+ calls por dia)
Text primeiro, depois call ("Maybe [Name]" trick)
Foco em peak interest capture
Goal: Buy or unsubscribe (não importa unsubscribes)
Lead Claiming Rules:
Só pode clamar se contactar imediatamente
Punição psicológica se não contactar
Prioriza velocidade vs. round robin
Competição entre closers incentivada
Communication Strategy:
Email, phone, Facebook Messenger
Preferência 50/50 phone vs. text
Adaptar ao canal preferido do lead
Vender via texto é válido (exemplo $35K watch)
ESTRUTURA:
Landing Page Format (ambos sistemas):
Headline
Video (mini webinar ou simples)
Calendar/Application OR Checkout button
Testimonials opcionais
Mesmo formato para ambas estratégias
Ad Creative:
Vídeo preferido vs. imagem
Melhor longevidade e escala
CTA: "Press button below for more info"
Pode usar mesma creative para ambos sistemas
Sales Team Structure:
Evitar round robin
Sistema de claiming competitivo
VA para cleanup administrativo
2-3 semanas máximo de follow-up por closer
Escalabilidade: 4 → 30+ closers quando necessário
Mindset Requirements:
Priorizar empresa vs. closer individual
Eliminar mentalidade "só scheduled calls são bons"
Velocidade > Conveniência
Agressividade profissional
"Want the deal but don't need the deal"
DADOS FINANCEIROS:
Mini Webinar 2.0:
ROI: $12M+ em 3.5 meses
Ticket médio: $25K-$100K
Aplicável: $5K+ mínimo
The Hydra - Own Boss Supply Co:
Input: $11 CPMs, $1-$3 CPL
Output: 3:1 ROAS imediato
Com email: 4.5+ ROAS
Revenue por email: $20-60K
Revenue por texto: $20-80K
The Hydra - Investment Offers:
54% leads claiming $100K liquid
$10 CPL para leads qualificados
Redução de $600-$1,000 → $10 CPL
The Hydra - Robert Case:
$1,900 gasto
$120K cash collected
$210K pipeline
Desde 28 de junho
Fence/Deck Case Study:
$12K ad spend
$622K revenue
1:4 conversão Facebook leads
$170K aumento mensal
15 min para appointment
Scaling Costs:
Start: $50-$200/dia
Evitar: $500/dia início (sobrecarrega time)
Target CPL: $5-$20
Ideal CPL: $1-$5 (com $10-$20 CPMs)
DURAÇÃO: ~2 horas (1:51:10)
FORMATO: Workshop presencial com apresentação de slides impressos e estratégias ao vivo
